name: GR 167: Prádena de Atienza to Cueva del Oso Trail
name_original: Mirador a la Cueva del Oso — trailrunning desde Prádena de Atienza
description: Jog this 6.0-mile (9.6 km) moderate trail from Prádena de Atienza to a viewpoint overlooking the Cueva del Oso, gaining 478 feet.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Prádena De Atienza are set within a serene mountain village in the Sierra Norte of Guadalajara. The region is characterized by the imposing Alto Rey mountain, which provides varied terrain from gentle slopes to challenging ascents. Pine forests and rocky landscapes offer natural shade and visually appealing running routes, while the Pelagallinas and Bornova rivers create picturesque riverside paths. The area also features rolling hills and pastures, providing diverse gradients for runners. Best jogging routes around Prádena De Atienza The most popular jogging route is GR 167: Prádena de Atienza to Cueva del Oso Trail, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers diverse landscapes and leads to the Bear Cave viewpoint. Another top favourite among local runners is Circular through the hills of Castillo and Padrastro from Atienza, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route winds through rolling hills and offers panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local runners also love the Prádena de Atienza – Sierra de Alto Rey loop from Prádena de Atienza, a 14.9 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Alto Rey, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Jogging around Prádena De Atienza is defined by mountain trails, pine forests, and riverside paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Prádena De Atienza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find around Prádena de Atienza?
A: Prádena de Atienza offers a diverse range of running routes, from moderate paths winding through rolling hills to challenging ascents in the Sierra de Alto Rey. You'll find trails through pine forests, rocky landscapes, and picturesque riverside paths along the Pelagallinas and Bornova rivers, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several moderate options. For a scenic and accessible run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032359021/circular-through-the-hills-of-castillo-and-padrastro-from-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular through the hills of Castillo and Padrastro from Atienza&lt;/a&gt;. This 7.4 km path offers panoramic views and a touch of medieval history, making it a good choice for a lighter jog or a family outing. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032365091/gr-167-pradena-de-atienza-to-cueva-del-oso-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 167: Prádena de Atienza to Cueva del Oso Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.6 km route with diverse landscapes.

Q: What are the most challenging running trails in the area?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the region offers demanding routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prádena de Atienza – Sierra de Alto Rey loop from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.9 km trail with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak (1,858 m) – Prádena de Atienza loop from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt;, covering 23.6 km with substantial climbing.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes around Prádena de Atienza?
A: Absolutely! Many of the running paths in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032359021/circular-through-the-hills-of-castillo-and-padrastro-from-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular through the hills of Castillo and Padrastro from Atienza&lt;/a&gt;. For a more strenuous circular option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prádena de Atienza – Sierra de Alto Rey loop from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on the running trails?
A: The running trails around Prádena de Atienza offer breathtaking scenic views. You'll encounter panoramic vistas from points like the Collado del Mediodía on Alto Rey Mountain, picturesque riverside paths along the Pelagallinas and Bornova rivers, and expansive views over rolling hills and pastures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Pelagallinas River&lt;/a&gt; are particularly noteworthy.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in natural and historical landmarks. Along your runs, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Atienza&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into medieval history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cueva del Oso&lt;/a&gt; (Bear Cave) is a notable natural feature. You can also explore the traditional 'black architecture' houses in villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4442873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atienza Village&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Prádena de Atienza?
A: Prádena de Atienza is enjoyable for jogging throughout much of the year. The pine forests provide natural shade, which can be beneficial during warmer months. The Pelagallinas River is noted for its fresh waters even in summer, offering a refreshing element to the natural surroundings. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, ideal for longer runs.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Prádena de Atienza dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary, the natural trails and open landscapes around Prádena de Atienza are generally welcoming for dogs, especially if kept on a leash. The tranquil environment and varied terrain make it a great place to run with your canine companion. Always ensure to follow local guidelines and clean up after your pet.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Prádena de Atienza area?
A: Komoot features a selection of 6 dedicated running routes around Prádena de Atienza, ranging from moderate to difficult. These routes are carefully curated to showcase the best of the region's diverse landscapes and natural beauty.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Prádena de Atienza?
A: Over 60 runners have used Komoot to explore the trails around Prádena de Atienza. They frequently praise the region's serene mountain environment, the varied terrain from gentle slopes to challenging ascents, and the picturesque paths through pine forests and along rivers. The tranquility and natural beauty are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any less crowded or 'hidden gem' running routes?
A: Given the serene and tranquil nature of Prádena de Atienza, many of its trails offer a peaceful running experience away from large crowds. While the main routes are popular, exploring some of the less-trafficked mountain paths and riverside trails can lead to a more secluded and intimate connection with nature. The extensive network of trails means you can often find quiet spots.
tips: Prádena de Atienza is nestled in a hidden bend of the Pelagallinas River before flowing into the Bornova River canyon, an important river line in the landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara. The streets of the town are on a slope to take advantage of the steep slopes of the valley at the foot of the Sierra de Alto Rey, an exclusive landscape of quartzite and slate ridges that close the eastern limits of the Central System.

The Pelagallinas river valley is a Natural Reserve of high environmental value within the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park due to the botanical diversity associated with fluvial ecosystems. The quartzite shoots that stand out in the slate relief of the mountains are striking and give a special geological value to the lonely mountain valley.
Cave tucked inside a rocky outcrop where legend has it that the great Cid Campeador took refuge one night.

faqs.question: How difficult is the trailrunning route to the Bear Cave viewpoint?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it follows a well-maintained mountain path, the mountainous terrain along the northern face of the Alto Rey mountain range requires a reasonable level of fitness, especially for trail running.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You'll start in the charming village of Prádena de Atienza, known for its unique 'black architecture'. Along the way, enjoy expansive Views of the Pelagallinas River and the majestic Alto Rey mountain. The highlight is reaching the View of Cueva del Oso (Bear Cave), which has local legends tied to El Cid.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash when trail running in natural areas, especially within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Prádena de Atienza?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Prádena de Atienza. You should be able to find parking within the village, which serves as the starting point for this route. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for accessing trails within the natural park. However, always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to trail run this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous landscape, spring and autumn are often ideal for trail running, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early morning start is recommended. Winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, requiring appropriate gear.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You will primarily be running on a well-maintained mountain path. The route traverses a mountainous landscape, offering varied ground that is generally suitable for trail running. Expect some ascents and descents typical of mountain trails.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route fully covers the trail named 'Entre el Sorbe y el Bornova. Sierra Norte de Guadalajara'. A small section also overlaps with 'Calle Moral de Arriba'.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This trail is approximately 5 km one way, making it a 10 km round trip. This indicates it is an out-and-back route, meaning you will return along the same path to your starting point in Prádena de Atienza.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is situated in a natural park, you might encounter various local wildlife. Keep an eye out for birds of prey, small mammals, and other typical mountain fauna. Always observe from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants in Prádena de Atienza?
faqs.answer: As the starting point, Prádena de Atienza is a charming village. While specific establishments are not listed, it's a good idea to check locally upon arrival for any small cafes or restaurants where you can refuel before or after your run.
